heading: Basic Cooking Principles
body: Raw food can cause food poisoning and reduce your health over time. Always cook meat before eating. Use a campfire, BBQ, kitchen oven, or microwave as your heat source. Cooking skill reduces the chance of burning food and unlocks more complex, nutritious recipes.

heading: Heat Sources
body: Campfires require wood fuel and must be lit with a lighter or matches. BBQs use propane and are commonly found in backyards. Kitchen ovens require the building to have active power. Campfires produce smoke — avoid using in enclosed areas or near windows to limit noise and visibility to zombies.

heading: Food Preservation
body: Fresh food spoils over time, especially in warm weather. Store perishables in a refrigerator while power lasts. Once power fails, cook or preserve food quickly. Salt can preserve meat for extended storage. Canned goods last indefinitely and should be stockpiled as a reliable long-term backup food source.

heading: Nutrition and Morale
body: Well-cooked meals restore more hunger and provide morale bonuses compared to eating raw or cold food. Boredom and low morale reduce all skill gain rates. Eating a variety of foods rather than the same meal repeatedly maintains morale. Stews and soups are the most efficient use of ingredients for restoring multiple stats at once.
